validation_output.node --test test/lifecycle-ownership.tes: exit 1; tests 8, pass 4, fail 4. Passing T023 safety cases: probable-owned refusal, unknown ownership refusal, external process refusal, stop_failed persistence/event evidence.

exit 1; tests 20, pass 16, fail 4. Failures: stop --json stops a verified-owned run and records stopped evidence; force-stop --json preserves the verified ownership gate; restart --json stops, waits for port release, and starts a new run in one transaction; restart --json records port_release_timeout when the stopped task leaves its port occupied.

concerns.test: stop --json stops a verified-owned run and records stopped evidence
concerns.details: The verified-owned process is stopped and persisted as stopped, but src/cli.js still emits the legacy global stop payload with process instead of the contract data.stopped[]/data.failed[] shape.

concerns.test: force-stop --json preserves the verified ownership gate
concerns.details: The force-stop command is not routed in src/cli.js, so the CLI returns unknown_command before reaching the ownership-gated runtime/action layer.

concerns.test: restart --json stops, waits for port release, and starts a new run in one transaction
concerns.details: The action layer has restartManagedRun with a single transaction id and port wait, but src/cli.js still uses the legacy restart route and emits process/readiness instead of stoppedRun/startedRun/transactionId.

concerns.test: restart --json records port_release_timeout when the stopped task leaves its port occupied
concerns.details: The runtime exposes waitForDeclaredPortsFree and the action-layer restart maps timeout to port_release_timeout, but the legacy src/cli.js restart route reports partial_failure.

recovery_hints: In T024, route launchdeck force-stop to the same stop action with force-owned semantics after verified ownership; do not let force grant ownership.
In T024, route global stop/restart through stopManagedRun/restartManagedRun from src/control-plane/actions.js and update JSON envelopes to the CLI contract shapes.
Keep the existing T023 runtime ownership gates intact: stored probable-owned, unknown, external, or stale-only evidence must continue to refuse before termination.
When wiring restart in T024, preserve the single transaction id from restartManagedRun and surface port_release_timeout directly instead of wrapping it as partial_failure.
